title: Colombia Votes for New Leaders and Congress
summary: In Colombia, people voted on Sunday to choose a new Congress and pick candidates for the presidential election in May. The election happened with high security because of concerns about violence in some rural areas. There was an incident where many people tried to cross the border from Venezuela illegally to vote, which officials called fraud. The president and defense minister said they stopped buses and are investigating. The president also questioned the election software, but the registrar said it is safe and checked by international observers. Over 3,000 candidates ran for 285 seats in Congress, and more than 41 million people could vote. This election helps decide who will lead Colombia next.

background_read: Colombia is a country in South America where people vote to choose their leaders. Elections are important for democracy, which means people have a say in how their country is run. Sometimes, there can be problems like cheating or violence during elections, so officials work to keep things fair and safe. International observers from groups like the European Union help watch elections to make sure they are honest. This article talks about a recent election in Colombia where voters picked candidates for Congress and the presidency.
Article_Structure: WHO: Colombians and officials like the president and defense minister
WHAT: Voting for a new Congress and presidential candidates, with incidents of illegal voting and fraud
WHERE: In Colombia, especially near the border with Venezuela
WHY: To choose leaders and ensure a fair election process

perspectives.description: They are working to stop fraud and ensure election security.
They are participating in democracy but face challenges like violence and cheating.
